title: Potential energy of a system of charges (intermediate)
description: Let's practice some problems to conceptually understand the potential energy of a system of charges.
topic_path: Electric potential energy, electric potential, and voltage
Potential energy of a system of charges (intermediate)

assessment_items.question_content:
An external agent slowly brings three charges, $q_1=-3\ \mu\text C$, $q_2=1\ \mu\text C$, and $q_3=1\ \mu\text C$, from infinity to the three vertices of an equilateral triangle.

[[☃ image 1]]

Each side of the triangle is $1\ \text m$ in length.

**What is the work done by the external agent in this process?**     
*Note: Write the answer in terms of $\text a$, where $\text a=9\times10^{-3}$.*

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
##Strategy

The potential energy of the system of three charges, $U$, is a **sum of the potential energies of the three charge-pairs**, $U_{12}$, $U_{23}$, and $U_{31}$, due to the principle of superposition.

[[☃ image 3]]

How is the work done by the external agent in assembling the system from inifnity, $W$, related to the potential energy, $U$?

##Solution

When the charges are infinitely separated, the potential energy of the system, $U_\infty=0$. The work done on the charges, $W$, in arranging them from infinity is stored as new potential energy. We have,

[[☃ image 1]]

$\begin{align}
W&=\Delta U\\\\
&=U-U_\infty=U\\\\
&=\redE{U_{12}}+\purpleD{U_{23}}+\blue{U_{31}}\\\\
&=\redE{\dfrac{kq_1q_2}{r_{12}}}+\purpleD{\dfrac{kq_2q_3}{r_{23}}}+\blue{\dfrac{kq_3q_1}{r_{31}}}\\\\
&=9\times10^9 \dfrac{\text{Vm}}{\text C} \left[\dfrac{-3\ \mu\text C\times1\ \mu\text C}{1\ \text m}+\dfrac{1\ \mu\text C\times1\ \mu\text C}{1\ \text m}+\dfrac{1\ \mu\text C\times(-3\ \mu\text C)}{1\ \text m}\right]\\\\
&=-5\times9\times10^{-3}\ \text{joules}\\\\
&=-5\text a\ \text{joules}
\end{align}$

The correct answer is:

$-5\text a\ \text {J}$

assessment_items.question_content:
An external agent slowly brings together three point charges, $q_1=1\ \text C$, $q_2=1\ \text C$, and $q_3=-1\ \text C$, from infinity to the configuration shown below.

[[☃ image 1]]

**What is the work done by the external agent in this process?**     
*Note: Write the answer in terms of $k$, where $k=\dfrac{1}{4\pi\epsilon_0}=9\times10^9\ \dfrac{\text{Vm}}{\text C}$.*

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
##Strategy

The potential energy of the system of three charges, $U$, is a **sum of the potential energies of the three charge-pairs**, $U_{12}$, $U_{23}$, and $U_{31}$, due to the principle of superposition.

[[☃ image 3]]

How is the work done by the external agent in assembling the system from inifnity, $W$, related to the potential energy, $U$?

##Solution

When the charges are infinitely separated, the potential energy of the system, $U_\infty=0$. The work done on the charges, $W$, in arranging them from infinity is stored as new potential energy. We have,

[[☃ image 1]]

$\begin{align}
W&=\Delta U\\\\
&=U-U_\infty=U\\\\
&=\redE{U_{12}}+\blue{U_{23}}+\purpleD{U_{31}}\\\\
&=\redE{\dfrac{kq_1q_2}{r_{12}}}+\blue{\dfrac{kq_2q_3}{r_{23}}}+\purpleD{\dfrac{kq_3q_1}{r_{31}}}\\\\
&=k\left[\dfrac{1\ \text C\times1\ \text C}{1\ \text m}+\dfrac{1\ \text C\times(-1\ \text C)}{1\ \text m}+\dfrac{1\ \text C\times(-1\ \text C)}{2\ \text m}\right]\\\\
&=-\dfrac{k}{2}\ \text{Joules}
\end{align}$

The correct answer is:

$-\dfrac{k}{2}\ \text J$

options.caption: The charges are located at three equally spaced collinear points.

assessment_items.question_content:
Two point charges, $q_1$ and $q_2$, both of magnitude, $2\ \text C$, are fixed $2\ \text m$ apart.

An external agent slowly brings a third charge, $q_3=-1\ \text C$, from infinity to the midpoint joining $q_1$ and $q_2$, as shown below.

[[☃ image 1]]

**What is the work done by the external agent in this process?**     
*Note: Write the answer in terms of $k$, where $k=\dfrac{1}{4\pi\epsilon_0}=9\times10^9\ \dfrac{\text{Vm}}{\text C}$.*

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
##Strategy

Initially, $q_1$ and $q_2$ are closely located but $q_3$ is infinitely far away. Let the potential energy of this system be $U_i$.

[[☃ image 1]]

Finally, we have all three charges located close to each other. Let the potential energy of this system be $U_f$.

How is the work done in assembling the final configuration, $W$, related to the change in potential energy, $\Delta U$, or, $U_f-U_i$?

##Solution

The work done by the external agent in introducing $q_3$ equals the change in potential energy of the system.  We have,

$\begin{align}
W&=\blue{U_f}-\redE{U_i}\\\\
&=\blue{U_{31}+U_{23}+}\cancel{\blue{U_{12}}}-\cancel{\redE{U_{12}}}\\\\
&=U_{31}+U_{23}\\\\
&=\dfrac{kq_3q_1}{r_{31}}+\dfrac{kq_2q_3}{r_{23}}\\\\
&=k\left[\dfrac{(-1\ \text C)\times 2\ \text C}{1\ \text m}+\dfrac{2\ \text C\times(-1\ \text C)}{1\ \text m}\right]\\\\
&=-4k\ \text{Joules}
\end{align}$

The correct answer is:

$-4k\ \text J$

assessment_items.question_content:
Two point charges, $q_1=-1\ \text C$ and $q_2=-2\ \text C$, are fixed $4\ \text m$ apart.

An external agent slowly brings a third charge, $q_3=2\ \text C$, from infinity to the midpoint joining $q_1$ and $q_2$, as shown below.

[[☃ image 1]]

**What is the work done by the external agent in this process?**     
*Note: Write the answer in terms of $k$, where $k=\dfrac{1}{4\pi\epsilon_0}=9\times10^9\ \dfrac{\text{Vm}}{\text C}$.*

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
##Strategy

Initially, $q_1$ and $q_2$ are closely located but $q_3$ is infinitely far away. Let the potential energy of this system be $U_i$.

[[☃ image 1]]

Finally, we have all three charges located close to each other. Let the potential energy of this system be $U_f$.

How is the work done in assembling the final configuration, $W$, related to the change in potential energy, $\Delta U$, or, $U_f-U_i$?

##Solution

The work done by the external agent in introducing $q_3$ equals the change in potential energy of the system.  We have,

$\begin{align}
W&=\blue{U_f}-\redE{U_i}\\\\
&=\blue{U_{31}+U_{23}+}\cancel{\blue{U_{12}}}-\cancel{\redE{U_{12}}}\\\\
&=U_{31}+U_{23}\\\\
&=\dfrac{kq_3q_1}{r_{31}}+\dfrac{kq_2q_3}{r_{23}}\\\\
&=k\left[\dfrac{2\ \text C\times (-1\ \text C)}{2\ \text m}+\dfrac{(-2\ \text C)\times2\ \text C}{2\ \text m}\right]\\\\
&=-3k\ \text{Joules}
\end{align}$

The correct answer is:

$-3k\ \text J$

assessment_items.question_content:
Two point charges, $q_1=1\ \text C$ and $q_2=2\ \text C$, are fixed at points, $\text A$ and $\text B$, respectively.

[[☃ image 1]]

An external agent slowly moves a negative point charge, $q_3=-1\ \text C$, from $\text P$ to $\text Q$.

**What is the work done by the external agent in this process?**     
*Note: Write the answer in terms of $k$, where $k=\dfrac{1}{4\pi\epsilon_0}=9\times10^9\ \dfrac{\text{Vm}}{\text C}$.*

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
##Strategy

Initially, $q_3$ is $2\ \text m$ from $q_1$ and $1\ \text m$ from $q_2$. Let the potential energy of the system be $U_i$.

[[☃ image 1]]

Finally, $q_3$ is $2\ \text m$ from both $q_1$ and $q_2$. Let the new potential energy of the system be $U_f$.

[[☃ image 2]]

How is the work done, $W$, in slowly moving $q_3$ from $\text P$ to $\text Q$, related to the change in potential energy, $\Delta U$ or $U_f-U_i$?

##Solution

Let's note that the distance between $q_1$ and $q_2$ ($r_{12}$) is fixed. 

The work done by the external agent in moving $q_3$ equals the change in potential energy of the system.

$\begin{align}
U_f&=k\left[\blueE{\dfrac{(-1\ \text C)\times 1\ \text C}{2\ \text m}}+\dfrac{(-1\ \text C)\times 2\ \text C}{2\ \text m}+\blueE{U_{12}}\right]\\\\
U_i&=k\left[\blueE{\dfrac{(-1\ \text C)\times 1\ \text C}{2\ \text m}}+\dfrac{(-1\ \text C)\times 2\ \text C}{1\ \text m}+\blueE{U_{12}}\right]
\end{align}$

where $U_{12}$ is the potential energy of the $\{q_1$, $q_2\}$ pair. Now, $W=U_f-U_i$, where the terms shaded blue cancel. We have,

$\begin{align}
W&=U_f-U_i\\\\
&=k\left[\dfrac{(-1\ \text C)\times 2\ \text C}{2\ \text m}-\dfrac{(-1\ \text C)\times 2\ \text C}{1\ \text m}\right]\\\\
&=k\ \text{Joules}
\end{align}$

The correct answer is:

$k\ \text J$

assessment_items.question_content:
Two point charges, $q_1=1\ \text C$ and $q_2=-1\ \text C$, are fixed at points, $\text A$ and $\text B$, respectively.

[[☃ image 1]]

An external agent slowly moves a negative point charge, $q_3=-2\ \text C$, from $\text P$ to $\text Q$.

**What is the work done by the external agent in this process?**     
*Note: Write the answer in terms of $k$, where $k=\dfrac{1}{4\pi\epsilon_0}=9\times10^9\ \dfrac{\text{Vm}}{\text C}$.*

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
##Strategy

Initially, $q_3$ is $2\ \text m$ from $q_1$ and $4\ \text m$ from $q_2$. Let the potential energy of the system be $U_i$.

[[☃ image 1]]

Finally, $q_3$ is $4\ \text m$ from both $q_1$ and $q_2$. Let the new potential energy of the system be $U_f$.

[[☃ image 2]]

How is the work done, $W$, in slowly moving $q_3$ from $\text P$ to $\text Q$, related to the change in potential energy, $\Delta U$ or $U_f-U_i$?

##Solution

Let's note that the distance between $q_1$ and $q_2$ ($r_{12}$) is fixed. 

The work done by the external agent in moving $q_3$ equals the change in potential energy of the system.

$\begin{align}
U_f&=k\left[\dfrac{\overbrace{(-2\ \text C)}^{q_3}\times \overbrace{1\ \text C}^{q_1}}{4\ \text m}+\dfrac{\overbrace{(-1\ \text C)}^{q_2}\times \overbrace{(-2\ \text C)}^{q_3}}{2\ \text m}+U_{12}\right]\\\\
U_i&=k\left[\dfrac{\overbrace{(-2\ \text C)}^{q_3}\times \overbrace{1\ \text C}^{q_1}}{2\ \text m}+\dfrac{\overbrace{(-1\ \text C)}^{q_2}\times \overbrace{(-2\ \text C)}^{q_3}}{2\ \text m}+U_{12}\right]\\\\
\end{align}$

where $U_{12}$ is the potential energy of the $\{q_1$, $q_2\}$ pair. We have,

$\begin{align}
W&=U_f-U_i\\\\
&=k\left[\dfrac{(-2\ \text C)\times 1\ \text C}{4\ \text m}-\dfrac{(-2\ \text C)\times 1\ \text C}{2\ \text m}\right]\\\\
&=\dfrac{k}{2}\ \text{Joules}
\end{align}$

The correct answer is:

$\dfrac{k}{2}\ \text J$

assessment_items.question_content:
Two point charges, $q_1=2\ \text C$ and $q_2=1\ \text C$, are fixed $1\ \text m$ apart. Another point charge, $q_3=-2\ \text C$, is initially located $1\ \text m$ away from both $q_1$ and $q_2$.

[[☃ image 1]]

An external agent slowly moves $q_3$ to infinity.

**What is the work done by the external agent in this process?**     
*Note: Write the answer in terms of $k$, where $k=\dfrac{1}{4\pi\epsilon_0}=9\times10^9\ \dfrac{\text{Vm}}{\text C}$.*

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
##Strategy

Initially, all the charges are separated by $1\ \text m$ from each other. Let the potential energy of the system be $U_i$.

[[☃ image 1]]

Finally, $q_3$ is isolated to infinity. The new potential energy, $U_f$, is only due to $q_1$ and $q_2$.

How is the work done, $W$, in slowly moving away $q_3$ related to the change in potential energy, $\Delta U$ or $U_f-U_i$?

##Solution

The work done by the external agent in moving $q_3$ equals the change in potential energy of the system.

$\begin{align}
U_f&=U_{12}\\\\
U_i&=U_{12}+U_{23}+U_{31}
\end{align}$

where $U_{12}$ is the potential energy of the fixed $\{q_1$, $q_2\}$ pair. We have,

$\begin{align}
W&=U_f-U_i\\\\
&=-[U_{23}+U_{31}]\\\\
&=-k\left[\dfrac{q_2q_3}{r_{23}}+\dfrac{q_3q_1}{r_{31}}\right]\\\\
&=-k\left[\dfrac{1\ \text C\times(-2\ \text C)}{1\ \text m}+\dfrac{(-2\ \text C)\times2\ \text C}{1\ \text m}\right]\\\\
&=6k\ \text{Joules}
\end{align}$

The correct answer is:

$6k\ \text J$

assessment_items.question_content:
Two point charges, $q_1=-3\ \text C$ and $q_2=3\ \text C$, are fixed $2\ \text m$ apart. Another point charge, $q_3=3\ \text C$, is initially located $2\ \text m$ away from both $q_1$ and $q_2$.

[[☃ image 1]]

An external agent slowly moves $q_3$ to infinity.

**What is the work done by the external agent in this process?**     
*Note: Write the answer in terms of $k$, where $k=\dfrac{1}{4\pi\epsilon_0}=9\times10^9\ \dfrac{\text{Vm}}{\text C}$.*

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
##Strategy

Initially, all the charges are separated by $2\ \text m$ from each other. Let the potential energy of the system be $U^i$.

[[☃ image 1]]

Finally, $q_3$ is isolated to infinity. The new potential energy, $U^f$, is only due to $q_1$ and $q_2$.

How is the work done, $W$, in slowly moving away $q_3$ related to the change in potential energy, $\Delta U$ or $U^f-U^i$?

##Solution

The work done by the external agent in moving $q_3$ equals the change in potential energy of the system.

$\begin{align}
U^f&=U^f_{12}\\\\
U^i&=U^i_{12}+U^i_{23}+U^i_{31}
\end{align}$

Since $q_1$ and $q_2$ are fixed, $U^f_{12}-U^i_{12}=0$. We have,

$\begin{align}
W&=U^f-U^i\\\\
&=-[U^i_{23}+U^i_{31}]\\\\
&=-k\left[\dfrac{q_2q_3}{r_{23}}+\dfrac{q_3q_1}{r_{31}}\right]\\\\
&=-k\left[\dfrac{3\ \text C\times3\ \text C}{2\ \text m}+\dfrac{3\ \text C\times(-3\ \text C)}{2\ \text m}\right]\\\\
&=0
\end{align}$

The correct answer is:

$0$
